work.name: LJK Laboratory - DAO Team
highlights: Reproduced and analyzed experiments from 'Optimal Transport for Domain Adaptation' (Courty et al., 2015)
Contributed to the open-source Julia ecosystem through a pull request to OptimalTransport.jl
Studied research literature on Monte Carlo methods, quasi-Monte Carlo integration, optimal transport theory, and their applications in machine learning
Supervised by Quoc-Tung Le and Matthieu Besançon

awards.title: National Excellencia Scholarship Competition - 2nd Place
awards.summary: Ranked 2nd nationwide in the Excellencia Excellence Scholarship selection process, Democratic Republic of the Congo

projects.name: QMClib - Monte Carlo and Quasi-Monte Carlo Library
projects.description: Open-source Python library implementing Monte Carlo and quasi-Monte Carlo algorithms for numerical integration, including Uniform sampling, Latin Hypercube Sampling, Halton, Sobol, and lattice-based methods.
highlights: Designed a modular framework for sampling methods, statistical analysis, convergence evaluation, and performance benchmarking
Validated through numerical experiments and comparative benchmarks against established quasi-Monte Carlo libraries
Applied to a machine learning risk estimation problem for high-dimensional integration tasks
